mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding month signals monetary accommodation that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.42% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.6% is historically low
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer demand strength and forward earnings visibility. Accelerating retail sales over the preceding month signal robust demand that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through momentum and earnings revision channels.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with RSAFS momentum (Spearman rho < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) indicates real economic capacity utilization and supply-side strength. When INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month, it signals robust productive capacity that supports corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite strong INDPRO growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer demand breadth across durable and non-durable sectors. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand strength that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction effect is stronger than either series alone because it rules out sector-specific noise.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both show positive month-over-month growth in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1139.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 513356.0 million dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC is not significantly higher than max(IC_HOUST, IC_RSAFS) (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market momentum. When PAYEMS growth accelerates while UNRATE remains stable or declines, it signals tight labor market conditions that support wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth > 200k and UNRATE unchanged or declining
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 151095.0 thousand, UNRATE at 3.6%
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage-growth support
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression controlling for individual series.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 3%) signals restrictive monetary policy in a high-inflation regime. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because equity valuations compress under persistent real rate pressure and earnings growth expectations decline.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d on CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of economic demand. When quarterly GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized, it signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional: it is strongest in early-to-mid cycle expansions and weakest in late-cycle or recessionary regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 18912.326 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in above-2% growth quarters are not significantly higher than below-2% quarters (t-test p > 0.10).
